name: Comp AI
version_status: PARTLY VERIFIED from current repo comparisons
what: Open-core compliance operations platform for policies, controls, evidence, and governance workflows.
solves: Compliance program management, evidence collection, control mapping, and operational governance workflows.
users: Security/compliance teams running structured control frameworks.
fit: Useful only if Hylono is actively building a formal compliance program. It is not an early operational necessity for the product stack itself.
runtime: Web platform with database, multiple app components, and evidence/control workflows.
components: Policy/control management, evidence workflows, user/admin surfaces, and integrations.
deploy: Self-host docs exist in repo; production requires careful access control and governance ownership.
deps: Database, storage, auth, mail, and process owners for controls/evidence.
auth: Local users and likely SSO options in enterprise contexts.
apis: App surfaces and integrations for evidence/control workflows.
security: Compliance tooling handles sensitive internal evidence and control data. Access controls and auditability matter.
setup_path: Do not prioritize until a formal compliance program has owners, frameworks, and evidence processes.
env: DB settings, app secrets, storage/auth/mail config, and service-specific env files.
persistence: Back up DB and evidence storage.
operations: Framework upkeep, evidence lifecycle, and integration maintenance matter more than deployment.
failure_points: Deploying compliance software before the organization is ready to operate a real compliance program.
integration: Could pair with Fides, Retraced, Wazuh, Infisical, and internal docs if governance becomes a strategic workstream.
verify: Confirm whether Hylono is actively pursuing frameworks that justify dedicated compliance operations tooling.
risk: Moderate to high process mismatch risk.
gdpr: Mostly internal governance data, but may contain sensitive evidence and access data.
recommendation: LATER / ONLY IF FORMAL COMPLIANCE PROGRAM IS ACTIVE
overlap: Partial overlap with Fides and internal docs/evidence systems, but different focus.
codex_read_first: Read the compliance strategy notes before recommending Comp AI.
codex_best_path: Only implement when there are named owners, frameworks, and evidence workflows to support.
codex_never_assume: Never assume software creates a compliance program by itself.
